past.predicted_context: During formative years, frequent exposure to mobile devices and a lack of structured, uninterrupted work periods cultivated a habit of seeking immediate dopamine hits from phone notifications.
past.contradiction: A strong desire to sustain four hours of deep work clashes with a well‑established habit of instantly reaching for the phone at the slightest distraction.
past.key_failure_point: The moment a notification appears, the automatic phone‑check interrupts the flow and reinforces the cycle.
past.origin_story: This pattern likely originated from early environments where constant digital stimuli were paired with reward, teaching the mind that focus must compete with instant gratification.

present.primary_blocker: Frequent phone checking and notification-driven interruptions
present.emotional_blocker: Anxiety and fear of missing out that drives compulsive phone use
present.weekly_cost_estimate: Estimated 9–11 hours per week lost to phone‑related distractions and task switching
present.physical_reframe: You likely feel a restless, jittery sensation in your chest and a light‑headed pull in your hands, as if your nervous system is on high alert—not laziness, but a stress response to perceived loss of information.

future.failure_simulation: If you keep letting your phone hijack your attention, the scattered hours will add up and you’ll never reach a solid four‑hour work block.
future.success_simulation: It’s September, and I’m already deep into my project, eyes fixed on the screen, the phone silent on the desk. For four hours straight I ride a wave of focus, ticking tasks off without a single glance at notifications. My mind feels sharp, the work flows, and the satisfaction of sustained concentration fuels the rest of my day.
future.impact_on_life: Sustained focus boosts my productivity, frees up evenings for personal pursuits, reduces stress, and restores confidence in my ability to tackle complex tasks.

integration.impact_statement: Your phone is stealing every minute, keeping you in an instant‑gratification loop that blocks any chance of a solid four‑hour work block. By forcing a tiny, safe failure—letting a single 30‑minute sprint slip if you check the phone—you’ll break that reflex, build real focus muscle, and move toward the 4‑hour stretch you crave.
integration.message_from_mentor: Stop talking about focus. Start a timer, lock the phone, and prove to yourself you can own the next half hour. Every second you win is a brick in the wall you’re building.

micro_task.title: 30‑Minute Phone‑Free Sprint
micro_task.description: Set a 30‑minute timer, place your phone face‑down in a bowl on the desk, open a blank document titled “Focus Sprint #1”, and write nonstop until the timer rings.
micro_task.reward: Immediate sense of control and proof that you can hold attention.

roadmap.expected_result: By month’s end you will have completed a 4‑hour work block prototype and have a reliable routine for extending focus in real time.

weeks.focus: Destroy the reflex to grab the phone
weeks.outcome: Complete a 30‑minute work sprint without unlocking the phone
weeks.win_condition: Finished 30 minutes of work with phone face‑down and no unlocks

days.action: Set a 30‑minute timer, put your phone in a bowl face‑down, turn the screen off, open a new document named “Focus Sprint #1”, write anything until the timer rings, then write down how many times you thought about the phone.
Repeat Day 1, then after the timer take a 2‑minute stretch and immediately start a second 30‑minute sprint using the same phone placement.
Do a 5‑minute warm‑up: read one paragraph of your work material, then launch a 30‑minute sprint exactly as Day 1.
Increase the timer to 35 minutes, keep the phone in the bowl, and write continuously until the timer ends.
Run a 30‑minute sprint, then record a 1‑minute voice note saying “I stayed focused today” and store it for later review.
Before your coffee, place the phone in another room, set a 30‑minute timer, and work on the same document without any interruptions.
Do two back‑to‑back 30‑minute sprints with a 5‑minute break in between; keep the phone in the bowl the whole time.

weeks.outcome: Complete a 45‑minute uninterrupted work session
weeks.win_condition: Finished 45 minutes of work with phone face‑down and no unlocks

days.action: Set a 45‑minute timer, put phone in bowl, start writing on “Focus Sprint #2” and keep going until the timer ends.
Same as Day 1, but after the sprint take a 3‑minute walk before closing the document.
Begin with a 2‑minute breathing reset, then a 45‑minute sprint, phone still in bowl.
Do a 45‑minute sprint, then write a single sentence summarizing what you achieved.
Place the phone on “Do Not Disturb”, set a 45‑minute timer, and work on any task you’ve been avoiding.
Morning: 45‑minute sprint with phone in a different room; note any urges and how you handled them.
Two 45‑minute sprints back‑to‑back with a 5‑minute stretch break; keep phone out of sight the entire day.

weeks.outcome: Finish a 2‑hour work session without checking the phone
weeks.win_condition: Completed 2 hours of work with phone on Do Not Disturb and no unlocks

days.action: Set two consecutive 60‑minute timers, put phone on Do Not Disturb, work straight through both intervals, only stand up for a 2‑minute stretch at the 60‑minute mark.
Repeat Day 1, but after the first hour write a brief 30‑second summary before starting the second hour.
Morning: place phone in another room, set a single 120‑minute timer, and work on the same document until it rings.
Do a 2‑hour sprint, then record a 30‑second audio note: “I held focus for two hours.”
Set two 55‑minute timers with a 5‑minute micro‑break in between; phone stays on Do Not Disturb.
Evening: after dinner, put phone on Airplane mode, set a 2‑hour timer, and finish a specific task chunk.
Two 1‑hour blocks separated by a 10‑minute walk; keep phone out of sight the whole day.

weeks.outcome: Complete your first 4‑hour uninterrupted work day
weeks.win_condition: Worked 4 hours straight (with only micro‑breaks) and phone locked away

days.action: Lock your phone in a drawer, set a 4‑hour timer, break it into two 2‑hour segments with a 5‑minute stretch in between, and work continuously until the timer ends.
Same as Day 1, but after each hour take a 2‑minute eye‑rest break; phone remains locked.
Morning: lock phone, set a 120‑minute timer, work, then a short 5‑minute break, followed by another 120‑minute timer; no phone checks.
Do a single 4‑hour sprint with the phone in a sealed box; after finishing, write a short reflection on energy levels.
Two 2‑hour blocks with a 10‑minute walk break; phone stays locked in a bag.
Evening: lock phone, set a 4‑hour timer, and finish the biggest pending task you have.
Celebrate: lock phone, run a 4‑hour sprint on any creative work, then reward yourself with a favorite low‑energy activity.

roadmap.expected_result: By month’s end you will sustain two 4‑hour deep‑work blocks per day, master task batching with energy peaks, complete a tangible deliverable, and operate 24‑hour without phone unlocks.

weeks.outcome: Complete two separate 4‑hour deep‑work sessions in a single day with zero phone interaction.
weeks.win_condition: Logged two distinct 4‑hour blocks and phone remained locked for the entire day.

days.action: Morning: lock phone in a zip‑lock bag, set Timer A for 4 hours (8‑am–12 pm). Work on Task A. After Timer A, take a 30‑minute active break, then set Timer B for 4 hours (12:30‑4:30 pm) on Task B. No phone unlocks.
Repeat Day 1 but swap tasks: start with writing a draft (Task C) then switch to data analysis (Task D). Record a brief log after each block.
Introduce a 5‑minute breathing reset before each block. Keep a water bottle nearby. No phone contact from 7 am to 5 pm.
Add a 2‑minute eye‑rest micro‑break every 45 minutes during both blocks. Use a timer app on a separate laptop.
Evening focus: start first 4‑hour block at 2 pm, finish by 6 pm. Afterward, write a 1‑minute voice note summarizing progress.
Weekend deep work: lock phone overnight, begin first block at 9 am, second block at 2 pm. Include a 10‑minute walk between blocks.
Reflection day: review logs from the week, identify the most productive task type, and adjust the next week’s task list accordingly.

weeks.outcome: Align work with natural energy peaks to achieve 6 hours of focused output using two 3‑hour blocks.
weeks.win_condition: Logged 6 hours of work, each block ≥ 3 hours, with phone locked for the entire day and tasks completed as batched.

days.action: Identify personal energy peak (e.g., 9‑12 am). Schedule Block 1 (3 hrs) during peak for high‑cognitive tasks, Block 2 (3 hrs) at 2‑5 pm for execution tasks. Phone stays in a locked drawer.
Batch similar tasks: Block 1 – research & outline; Block 2 – coding & implementation. No email or messaging checks.
Add a 10‑minute nutrition break between blocks (protein snack, hydrate). Record energy levels before and after each block.
Introduce a 3‑minute visualization ritual at the start of each block to prime focus.
Swap block order to test flexibility: start with execution tasks at peak, then move research to afternoon.
Weekend: extend each block to 3.5 hours while maintaining a 30‑minute break. Verify phone remained untouched.
Compile a summary of output volume (pages, lines of code, etc.) and compare against baseline from Week 5.

weeks.outcome: Execute a full 24‑hour phone‑free day while delivering at least 2 hours of deep work.
weeks.win_condition: No phone unlocks recorded for 24 hours and ≥ 2 hours of documented deep work completed.

days.action: Place phone in a sealed box with a timed lock for 24 hours. Begin the day with a 15‑minute meditation, then start a 2‑hour deep‑work block on the highest‑priority project.
Repeat the phone‑in‑box routine, but split deep work into two 1‑hour blocks (morning & afternoon) to test intermittent focus.
Add analog tools: use paper notebook for ideas, a timer on the kitchen clock for intervals. No digital notifications allowed.
Incorporate a 20‑minute outdoor walk before the first block to boost alertness. Maintain phone lock throughout.
Extend deep work to 3 hours total (1 hr + 2 hr). Log any urges to check the phone and how you redirected them.
Weekend immersion: stay phone‑free for the entire Saturday. Complete a 4‑hour creative sprint, then take a low‑energy leisure activity (reading) without digital devices.
Reflect: write a 500‑word journal entry on mental clarity, productivity gains, and any challenges faced during the week.

weeks.outcome: Deliver a concrete, shareable deliverable (e.g., 5‑page report or prototype) within a single 4‑hour block and conduct a performance review.
weeks.win_condition: Deliverable completed, exported, and reviewed; final performance metrics logged and compared to Month 1 baseline.

days.action: Plan the deliverable: outline sections, set success criteria, and lock phone in a drawer. Execute a 4‑hour sprint to finish the first draft.
Edit and polish the draft within a second 4‑hour block. No email or messaging interruptions.
Export the deliverable (PDF/PNG), share with a peer for feedback, and log the time spent on revisions.
Conduct a 30‑minute performance review: compare total deep‑work hours, output volume, and win‑condition success rates against Month 1.
Create a visual dashboard of key metrics (hours, tasks completed, phone‑free minutes) using a spreadsheet.
Celebrate the milestone with a low‑energy activity (e.g., walk in nature) while keeping the phone on Airplane mode.
Write a 1‑page action plan for Month 3, focusing on scaling output to 8‑hour blocks and integrating collaborative work.

roadmap.expected_result: By month’s end you will reliably sustain one 8‑hour deep‑work block per day, integrate ultra‑disciplined collaborative sprints with ≤2 phone unlocks, double your output metrics versus Month 2, and produce a concrete scaling blueprint for Month 4 while keeping daily phone unlocks under 5 minutes.

weeks.outcome: Complete a single uninterrupted 8‑hour deep‑work session each day, delivering a functional prototype module for the core project.
weeks.win_condition: Logged 8 continuous hours of work, phone remained locked for the full block, and the prototype module passed a basic functional test.

days.action: Morning: place phone in a sealed box with a 24‑hour timed lock. Set an analog kitchen timer for 8 hours (7 am‑3 pm). Begin work on Core Module A. No digital interruptions.
Repeat the 8‑hour block, this time focusing on Core Module B. Insert a 5‑minute standing stretch every hour using a wristwatch timer.
Add a 10‑minute nutrition break at hour 4 (protein snack, hydration). Record energy level before and after the break in a paper journal.
Start block at 6 am to test early‑day peak. Use a red‑light lamp for eye‑strain reduction. No phone contact until 3 pm.
Introduce a 2‑minute breathing reset at the top of each hour. Continue working on Module C, aiming for a compile‑ready state.
Weekend deep work: invite an accountability partner to observe (via video call scheduled *after* the block). Complete a full 8‑hour sprint on integration testing.
Reflection: tally total hours, log phone‑free minutes, and write a 300‑word journal entry on mental stamina and any urge‑management tactics used.

weeks.outcome: Deliver a joint feature with a teammate while restricting phone unlocks to two 15‑minute windows per day.
weeks.win_condition: Feature merged and passing CI tests; phone unlock count ≤2 and total unlock time ≤5 minutes recorded for the week.

days.action: Define communication windows: 9:30‑9:45 am and 4:00‑4:15 pm via Slack. Lock phone in drawer; use a separate laptop for all messaging during windows.
Sprint on collaborative API endpoint (30 % of feature). Work in a 3‑hour block, then a 3‑hour block after lunch, respecting windows.
Implement unit tests for the API. No phone access until after the second communication window.
Peer review session scheduled at 4:05 pm (within window). Afterwards, finalize documentation in a 2‑hour deep‑work slot.
Integrate feedback and push to repository. Conduct a 30‑minute offline code walk‑through using printed diagrams.
Weekend: conduct a live demo with the teammate at 10 am (pre‑agreed window). Keep phone locked for the rest of the day.
Metrics review: count unlocks, total collaborative output (lines of code, test coverage), and write a brief success‑failure analysis.

weeks.focus: Digital Minimalism Mastery & Energy Cycle Optimization
weeks.outcome: Align every deep‑work block with personal circadian peaks, achieve a 20 % increase in output versus Week 9, and operate a phone‑free micro‑environment for 6 hours daily.
weeks.win_condition: Output metrics (pages, code lines, or prototype iterations) exceed Week 9 baseline by ≥20 %; phone‑free micro‑environment logged for at least 6 hours each day.

days.action: Track energy via a paper chart for the morning peak (8‑11 am). Schedule a 4‑hour block during this peak; lock phone in a zip‑lock bag.
Afternoon peak (2‑5 pm) dedicated to execution tasks. Use a mechanical timer for 25‑minute focus intervals.
Introduce a 15‑minute sunlight exposure ritual before each block to boost alertness. Keep phone out of sight.
Swap task types between peaks to test flexibility and record productivity impact in a handwritten log.
Add a 5‑minute cold‑shower burst after the morning block to reset nervous system, then resume work on refinement tasks.
Weekend: create a “digital‑free cabin” by moving work to a park bench with a portable solar charger for laptop only; phone remains sealed.
Compile weekly metrics, calculate % increase vs Week 9, and draft a 2‑page insight report on energy‑aligned productivity.

weeks.outcome: Produce a comprehensive 6‑page scaling report, automate the phone‑lock routine, and set concrete goals for Month 4.
weeks.win_condition: Report completed, automation script tested successfully (phone locked for 8 hours automatically), and next‑month roadmap documented and approved.

days.action: Outline the scaling report: sections on deep‑work architecture, collaboration protocols, energy optimization, and automation plan.
Write Sections 1‑2 (deep‑work architecture & collaboration) in a focused 3‑hour block; phone locked in drawer.
Write Sections 3‑4 (energy optimization & automation) using another 3‑hour block; test a simple Bash script that locks the phone via Android ADB.
Run the automation script for a trial 8‑hour period, record any failures, and adjust parameters.
Finalize the report, add visual dashboards of monthly metrics, and export as PDF.
Peer review of the report via scheduled 15‑minute video call (within communication window). Incorporate feedback.
Create a concrete Month 4 action plan (goal list, weekly themes, phone‑free protocols) and celebrate completion with a nature walk, phone on airplane mode.

roadmap.theme: Scale & Deploy – From Prototype to Market with Zero Distraction
roadmap.expected_result: Launch a production‑ready MVP, secure three early adopters, process the first revenue transaction, automate deployment and monitoring, and deliver a 5‑page scaling blueprint while keeping daily phone unlocks at two or fewer.

weeks.outcome: Produce a production‑ready MVP, implement a CI/CD pipeline, and deploy the app to a live environment without manual phone interaction.
weeks.win_condition: MVP passes user‑acceptance test, deployment script runs end‑to‑end successfully, and total phone unlocks for the week ≤ 2.

days.action: Seal phone in a timed lockbox (24 h). Review MVP checklist on paper and draft the deployment Bash script outline on a notebook.
Set up GitHub Actions CI pipeline on the laptop; run a local dry‑run. Phone stays locked in a drawer.
Provision staging environment via Terraform scripts; record all commands in a handwritten log.
Execute automated unit and integration tests through the CI script; verify zero failures. Phone remains sealed.
Run the one‑click production deployment script; monitor logs for 2 hours and note any manual interventions.
Weekend audit: run an offline OWASP security scan, document findings on paper, and keep phone on airplane mode.
Reflection: write a 300‑word journal on automation confidence, tally phone‑free minutes (target 0 unlocks), and outline next week’s priorities.

weeks.outcome: Sign up at least three qualified early adopters, collect actionable feedback, and iterate the MVP accordingly.
weeks.win_condition: Three signed agreement emails or PDFs received, feedback logged, and total phone unlocks for the week ≤ 2.

days.action: Print outreach email template; schedule two 15‑minute phone‑free outreach windows (09:00‑09:15, 16:00‑16:15) using the laptop only.
Send personalized email to Target 1; conduct a brief follow‑up call via web‑based VOIP after the second window; log response in a paper journal.
Email Target 2; schedule a demo meeting for the next day; keep phone locked away.
Run live demo for Target 2 via web conference on the laptop; record the session and capture feedback on printed sheets.
Email Target 3; negotiate terms and capture a signed PDF agreement using the laptop’s e‑signature tool.
Weekend: draft a short case‑study for Early Adopter 1 using only laptop and paper; phone stays in airplane mode.
Metrics review: count signed agreements (≥3), verify phone unlock count (≤2), and write a brief success‑failure analysis.

weeks.outcome: Integrate a payment gateway, process the first real transaction, and deploy a live KPI dashboard tracking core metrics.
weeks.win_condition: First real payment successfully processed, KPI dashboard live with real‑time data, and phone unlocks for the week ≤ 2.

days.action: Integrate Stripe (or equivalent) payment gateway in the backend; test with sandbox credentials; phone locked in drawer.
Create automated invoice generation script; run a test transaction and verify receipt email delivery.
Design a KPI dashboard (Grafana/Metabase) showing Daily Active Users, Revenue, Latency, and Error Rate; connect to live database.
Deploy the dashboard to a public URL; monitor for 1 hour, note any alerts, and adjust queries as needed.
Process the first real payment from an early adopter; confirm receipt in the dashboard and log revenue figure.
Weekend: run performance profiling on the app, document bottlenecks on paper, and keep phone on airplane mode.
Review KPI data versus baseline; write a 2‑page insight report; ensure phone unlock count remains ≤2.

weeks.outcome: Conduct a full retrospective, identify top friction points, and produce a 5‑page scaling blueprint for Month 5+ while maintaining ultra‑low distraction.
weeks.win_condition: Blueprint completed and approved, average daily phone unlocks across the month ≤ 2, and overall output increase ≥ 30 % versus Month 3 baseline.

days.action: Gather all metrics from Weeks 13‑15 into a master spreadsheet; print for tactile review.
Run a 30‑minute retrospective video call with accountability partner (scheduled after work block); phone stays locked.
Identify the three biggest friction points; create actionable items with deadlines on a paper Kanban board.
Draft the 5‑page scaling blueprint covering market expansion, hiring plan, automation upgrades, and risk mitigation.
Share the blueprint via a shared document for mentor feedback; incorporate comments without using the phone.
Finalize blueprint, obtain self‑sign‑off checklist, and celebrate with a 2‑hour nature hike; phone set to airplane mode.
Monthly audit: calculate average daily phone unlocks, total output increase vs. Month 3, verify ≥30 % uplift, and write final journal entry.

roadmap.expected_result: Acquire 1,000 active users, generate $10,000 ARR, launch two strategic partnerships with live integrations, deploy an AI‑driven recommendation engine that lifts conversion by 15%, and keep daily phone unlocks at one or fewer.

weeks.outcome: Sign two partnership agreements, integrate partner APIs into the product, and generate joint traffic.
weeks.win_condition: Two signed MoU PDFs, live API integration verified, and total phone unlocks for the week <= 1.

days.action: Research target partners, create outreach deck on laptop, and seal phone in a timed lockbox (24h).
Send personalized partnership proposals via email from laptop only; schedule 15‑minute video call using web conference.
Conduct first partnership video meeting, capture notes on paper, and keep phone locked away.
Draft integration technical specification, print and file in a binder.
Implement partner API sandbox integration on development environment; log commands in a handwritten notebook.
Run end‑to‑end automated tests for the integration; phone set to airplane mode.
Review integration status, obtain signed MoU via e‑signature tool on laptop, and tally phone unlock count.

weeks.outcome: Launch paid acquisition campaigns, acquire at least 500 new sign‑ups, and deploy a referral system.
weeks.win_condition: ≥500 new user registrations, CAC within target, and total phone unlocks for the week <= 1.

days.action: Design paid ad creatives and copy on laptop; lock phone in drawer.
Set up ad campaign budgets and tracking pixels via web dashboard; schedule launch time.
Launch campaigns, monitor spend and CTR in real‑time dashboard; phone remains sealed.
Analyze early performance data, adjust targeting and bids; document changes on paper.
Implement referral program code, generate share links, and run internal smoke test.
Run weekend A/B test on landing page variants; record results offline.
Consolidate acquisition metrics, verify ≥500 new sign‑ups, and record phone unlock count.

weeks.outcome: Deploy an AI‑driven recommendation engine, improve conversion by 15%, and launch a real‑time analytics dashboard.
weeks.win_condition: Recommendation engine live, conversion uplift >=15%, and total phone unlocks for the week <= 1.

days.action: Research recommendation algorithms and select model; document decision on laptop.
Build data pipeline for user behavior logs using Python scripts; phone locked in drawer.
Train AI model on historical data, evaluate precision, and store metrics in notebook.
Deploy model to production via CI/CD pipeline; run smoke tests to verify output.
Integrate model into app UI, monitor conversion impact, and note uplift percentage.
Create real‑time analytics dashboard (Grafana) showing conversion, DAU, and revenue; phone on airplane mode.
Review dashboard data, confirm conversion uplift >=15%, finalize documentation, and tally phone unlocks.

weeks.outcome: Reach $10,000 ARR, automate billing and support workflows, and produce SOPs for sustained scaling.
weeks.win_condition: $10k ARR achieved, ≥80% core processes automated, and total phone unlocks for the week <= 1.

days.action: Audit current revenue streams, identify upsell opportunities, and outline plan on paper.
Implement automated billing via subscription service API; test with sandbox credentials.
Launch upsell offers to existing users via email campaign; track acceptance rates on laptop.
Set up AI‑powered support chatbot, configure ticket triage workflows, and run pilot tests.
Measure ARR, confirm $10,000 threshold reached, and record revenue figure.
Draft SOPs for deployment, billing, and support; print and bind into a reference manual.
Final audit: verify ≥80% processes automated, average daily phone unlocks <=1, and write reflective journal entry.

roadmap.theme: Dominance & Resilience: From Startup to Enterprise Powerhouse
roadmap.expected_result: Close $50k MRR, onboard 5,000 active users, launch an enterprise tier with $20k ARR, secure 3 strategic channel partners, and maintain zero phone unlocks for the entire month.

weeks.outcome: Sign three enterprise contracts worth a combined $20k ARR and set up a dedicated sales funnel.
weeks.win_condition: Three signed contracts (PDF), enterprise funnel live, and total phone unlocks for the week = 0.

days.action: Identify top 10 target enterprises, compile a value‑prop deck on laptop, and lock phone in a timed lockbox (48h).
Personalize outreach emails using mail merge on laptop; schedule 30‑minute discovery calls via web calendar.
Conduct first discovery call, take notes on paper, and keep phone sealed in drawer.
Prepare custom proposal PDFs, print and hand‑deliver to decision‑makers (or use secure email client on laptop).
Negotiate terms in a 15‑minute video conference (web only), record agreement points in notebook.
Finalize contracts, capture e‑signatures on laptop, and store signed PDFs in encrypted folder.
Update CRM with pipeline status, verify three contracts signed, tally phone unlock count (must be 0).

weeks.outcome: Launch localized versions for two new regions, acquire 1,000 users per region, and establish local channel affiliates.
weeks.win_condition: Two localized product releases live, ≥2,000 new users acquired, affiliate agreements signed, and phone unlocks = 0.

days.action: Select target regions, research localization requirements on laptop, and place phone in a lockbox (24h).
Translate UI strings, update locale files offline, and test on staging environment without phone.
Deploy region‑specific builds to production, monitor rollout via dashboard on laptop.
Identify 5 potential local affiliates per region, draft partnership briefs on paper.
Reach out to affiliates via email, schedule 15‑minute intro calls using web video; keep phone locked.
Negotiate affiliate revenue share, capture signed MoU PDFs on laptop.
Verify user sign‑up numbers in analytics, confirm ≥2,000 new users, and log phone unlock count (0).

weeks.outcome: Expose core functionality via a public API, onboard 5 third‑party developers, and generate $5k incremental revenue.
weeks.win_condition: API docs published, 5 developers with active keys, $5k revenue recorded, and phone unlocks = 0.

days.action: Design API spec for core recommendation engine on laptop, lock phone in drawer.
Implement API endpoints in sandbox, write automated tests, and log results on paper.
Deploy API gateway, set rate limits, and publish Swagger docs on internal site.
Create developer onboarding flow (sign‑up, key generation) using only laptop tools.
Reach out to 10 potential dev partners via email, invite them to beta test the API.
Onboard first 5 developers, provide sandbox keys, and collect usage metrics offline.
Invoice beta developers for usage, confirm $5k revenue, and verify phone unlock count (0).

weeks.outcome: Codify SOPs for all core processes, implement a weekly deep‑focus ritual, and achieve a full month of zero phone unlocks.
weeks.win_condition: All SOPs version‑controlled, deep‑focus ritual executed 4×, phone unlocks for the week = 0, and a reflective performance report completed.

days.action: Audit existing SOPs, consolidate into a single Git repository, lock phone in timed lockbox (72h).
Write detailed runbooks for sales, engineering, and support; print and bind for offline reference.
Design a 90‑minute deep‑focus block (Pomodoro ×6) schedule, implement with a laptop timer, no phone.
Conduct first deep‑focus block, work on high‑impact backlog item, record progress in notebook.
Repeat deep‑focus block targeting a different department, capture outcomes on paper.
Hold a team retrospective (video conference via laptop), document improvement actions, and lock phone away.
Compile monthly performance report (ARR, users, contracts, API revenue), verify phone unlocks = 0, and celebrate with a digital‑detox reward.
